anchors_consulted: lean-tensor-categories Mathlib upstream cycle (Phase 5o Wave 4 + Wave 5)

figures.name: i2_fig1_categorical_hierarchy.png

physics.notes: Hasse diagram correctly encodes the 7-tier categorical hierarchy. MonoidalCategory and BraidedCategory occupy the lower (Mathlib substrate) band; Balanced -> Pivotal -> Ribbon and Fusion form the two parallel routes that converge at ModularTensorData (the apex). Edge directions correct (subclass arrows pointing up/towards extension).

style.notes: Two-band coloring achieved (steel-blue Mathlib substrate vs amber library-original); colorblind-safe; clean layout; left-side annotations 'Library originals (this work)' and 'Mathlib substrate' clearly labeled. Sans-serif diagram typography appropriate for schematic.

figures.notes: Figure title 'Categorical hierarchy: Monoidal -> ... -> Modular' matches caption phrasing in §3. The two-route convergence (balanced->pivotal->ribbon AND fusion -> ModularTensorData) matches caption text exactly. SphericalCategory.lean is mentioned in the §3 prose as 22 declarations but does NOT appear as a separate node in fig 1; the round-1 advisory recommended it. Acceptable since SphericalCategory is treated as an aspect of the Pivotal/Ribbon chain in the body, but a strengthening pass could add it explicitly. Minor.

figures.name: i2_fig2_module_dependencies.png

issues: Sankey node labels render with small font; on the LHS (instance modules) labels like 'SU(3)_2 (F-symbols)' and 'SU(2)_3/SU2kSMatrix' are legible but require zoom at print scale. Acceptable at 0.95\linewidth in a single-column journal but borderline in two-column.

physics.notes: Four-tier architectural layering correct: amber instance modules (left) -> berry Hopf-algebra modules (mid-left, e.g. Uqsl2Hopf, Uqsl2AffineHopf, Uqsl3Hopf, QuantumGroupHopf) -> steel-blue number-field modules (mid-right, QCyc16, QSqrt2, QCyc5) -> right-most categorical-core modules (KLinearCategory, RibbonCategory, FusionCategory, SphericalCategory) plus the QSqrt5/QCyc5SqrtPhi/QCyc15/QCyc5Ext peripheral number-fields. Flow direction (instances -> foundations) matches paper §7 prose.

issues: The disconnected VerifiedJackknife island recommended in round-1 I2-FIG-2 rationale is NOT shown in this figure. VerifiedJackknife is covered separately in I2-FIG-4, so this is acceptable, but the round-1 advisory anticipated a single 'two pieces of independent infrastructure' visual. Since fig 4 satisfies the same constraint via a dedicated diagram, this is not a blocker.
style.notes: Plotly Sankey; colorblind-safe palette respected (amber/berry/steel-blue); no red/green confusion.

figures.notes: Caption (paper line 611-621) accurately describes the 4-tier architecture (categorical core / Hopf / number-fields / instances) and the Sankey direction. Caption claims '28 modules' which matches the 28-module count cited in the abstract.

figures.name: i2_fig3_mtc_instances.png

rendering.notes: Plotly Table renders cleanly. 8 instance rows + header row. Steel-blue ✓ for shipped, amber 'partial' callouts (SU(2)_1 F-symbols, SU(2)_2 F-symbols, Fibonacci Verlinde), grey ✗ for not-yet-formalized. Number-field column shows Q, Q(√2), Q(ζ_n), Q(√2, e^{iπ/8}), Q(ζ_5)[√φ] correctly per §5/§6 narrative.

physics.notes: Simple-object counts correct: SU(2)_k has rank k+1 (rows show 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 for k=1..5 — correct); SU(3)_2 has rank 6 (correct); Ising has rank 3 (correct); Fibonacci has rank 2 (correct). Quantum dimensions: SU(2)_1 (1,1) — correct (trivial fusion); SU(2)_2 (1, √2, 1) — correct; SU(2)_3 (1, φ, φ, 1) — correct (golden-ratio dimensions emerge at k=3). Fibonacci (1, φ) — correct.

issues: The figure's vertical extent leaves substantial blank space below the table (table fills only the upper ~30% of the canvas). For JOSS submission this wastes column-inches and would benefit from cropping or a tighter Plotly figure size. Cosmetic only.
style.notes: Steel-blue / amber / grey palette consistent with project conventions.

figures.notes: Caption (paper line 513-522) correctly describes column structure. Note: caption says 'Verlinde-formula' but figure header says 'Verlinde verified' — minor copy-edit consistency issue but not load-bearing. Round-1 I2-FIG-3 recommendation also asked for a parallel VerifiedJackknife row 'so both pieces of infrastructure are surveyed in one visual' — that suggestion was NOT incorporated, but I2-FIG-4 covers VerifiedJackknife independently, so this is a stylistic choice not a blocker.

figures.name: i2_fig4_jackknife_dependencies.png

rendering.notes: Clean dependency micro-graph. 4 amber project theorems (jackknifeVariance_nonneg, autocovariance_zero_nonneg, intAutocorrTime_uncorrelated, intAutocorrTime_ge_half) at the top tier; 4 steel-blue Mathlib lemmas (mul_nonneg, Finset.sum_nonneg, sq_nonneg, mul_self_nonneg) at the bottom tier; one grey project def (autocovariance) shown right of center. Legend present in upper-right. Edge connectivity correct: jackknifeVariance_nonneg -> mul_nonneg + Finset.sum_nonneg + sq_nonneg; autocovariance_zero_nonneg -> Finset.sum_nonneg + sq_nonneg + mul_self_nonneg; intAutocorrTime_ge_half -> mul_self_nonneg via Finset.sum_nonneg; intAutocorrTime_uncorrelated -> autocovariance (def).

physics.notes: Theorem -> lemma reductions match VerifiedJackknife.lean structure (lines 50-58 jackknifeVariance_nonneg, 82-86 autocovariance_zero_nonneg, 109-114 intAutocorrTime_uncorrelated, 119-124 intAutocorrTime_ge_half — all line ranges agree with paper §2 prose).

issues: Per round-1 I2-ADV-2 (caption ↔ test-name traceability), the figure caption (paper line 252-264) claims 'each project theorem is annotated with the corresponding test function in tests/test_jackknife.py'. The figure does NOT visibly show test-name badges on each theorem node. Either: (a) revise caption to drop the per-node-annotation claim and instead refer prose-readers to §2 for test references, or (b) re-author figure with test-name badges (e.g. 'jackknifeVariance_nonneg' node carries a small 'test_jackknife_variance_nonneg' badge). Option (a) is simpler; option (b) is what the round-1 advisory anticipated. Either is acceptable; current state is mildly inconsistent.
style.notes: Sage-green / amber / steel-blue palette consistent with project conventions. Two-tier visual lanes (theorems above, lemmas below) match the round-1 style note.

figures.caption_match_notes: Caption claims per-theorem test annotation but figure does not show test names per node. See style.issues above.

figures.name: i2_fig5_mathlib_upstream_flow.png

issues: CRITICAL: Node labels are heavily clipped. Visible label fragments include 'athlib Zulip introd...', 'ool acceptance dis...', 'R strategy discus...', '12 + Co putable...', 'alCateg ry + Ribb...', 'ularBial gebra + R...', 'tances (SU(2)..'. The labels appear to be rendered INSIDE the squares/circles where the node shape is too small to contain the full text. The reader cannot determine which node is R1 vs R2 vs R3, nor identify which atomic PR is which (PR-1 QSqrt2+ComputableAdjoinRoot, PR-2 Pivotal+Ribbon, PR-3 Quasitriangular+RibbonHopf, PR-4 MTC instances). The diamond fallback nodes 'software-only JOSS' and 'update : etrofit upd...' are similarly clipped.

Recommendation: re-author with one of (i) increase node size by ~2x, (ii) place labels OUTSIDE node shapes (e.g. above/below), (iii) use abbreviated labels inside nodes with a legend table mapping abbreviation -> full description, or (iv) switch from circles/squares to wider rectangles with text-fitted bounding boxes.

physics.notes: Despite label clipping, the structural flow is correct: 3 R-gates (steel-blue squares) -> 4 PR nodes (amber circles) -> diamond decision -> 2 fallback nodes (grey diamonds). Annotation 'Fallback engages if Mathlib AI-content acceptance is delayed > 6 months' visible at the bottom and matches §7.4 prose. The 7-step flow + fallback branch is the structural content the round-1 advisory anticipated.

issues: Label-clipping is a hard rendering bug, not a style preference. Fix required before submission.
style.notes: Legend in upper-left correctly distinguishes R-gate (steel-blue) / atomic PR (amber) / software-only fallback (grey diamond).

figures.caption_match_notes: Caption text (paper line 692-702) is accurate; the figure encodes the right structure but the rendering renders the labels unreadable. Caption-figure semantic match is OK; the FAIL is purely a rendering bug.

summary: Round-2 review of 5 newly-authored I2 figures. Four of five pass with minor advisories (i2_fig1 categorical hierarchy clean PASS; i2_fig2 module deps PASS with sub-pixel-density advisory; i2_fig3 MTC instances PASS with cosmetic blank-space advisory; i2_fig4 jackknife deps PASS with caption-vs-figure annotation inconsistency advisory). One figure FAILS: i2_fig5 mathlib upstream flow has heavily clipped node labels rendering R1/R2/R3 gates and PR-1..4 nodes unreadable. Overall verdict: issues_found (yellow). The two P0 figures (categorical hierarchy + module dependency) are publication-ready. The P1 figures (MTC table + VerifiedJackknife graph) are publication-ready with minor advisories. The P2 figure (Mathlib upstream flow) requires a rendering revision before JOSS submission. Bundle stage9_status set to yellow (advisories + 1 fail on lowest-priority figure; no P0/P1 blocker).

blockers.description: Node labels are clipped beyond legibility. Reader cannot identify R-gates or atomic PRs from the figure alone. Re-author the figure with larger nodes, external labels, or rectangular text-fitted shapes. Until fixed, the figure communicates only the structural flow (gates -> PRs -> fallback) without the substantive content (which gate, which PR). Per JOSS submission standards, unreadable labels in a process-flow diagram are a hard FAIL.
blockers.remediation: Re-author papers/I2/figures/i2_fig5_mathlib_upstream_flow.png with one of: (a) larger nodes (~2x current size); (b) labels placed above/below nodes rather than inside; (c) abbreviated in-node labels + legend table; (d) text-fitted rectangular shapes. Re-invoke physics-qa:figure-reviewer for verification.

advisories.description: Caption (paper line 252-264) claims 'each project theorem is annotated with the corresponding test function in tests/test_jackknife.py' but the figure shows no per-node test-name badges. Either revise caption to drop the per-theorem annotation claim and refer to §2 prose for test references (simpler), OR re-author the figure with test-name badges per theorem node (what round-1 I2-ADV-2 anticipated). Current state is a mild caption-figure mismatch; not load-bearing for the figure's structural content but should be reconciled before final submission.
advisories.remediation: Edit caption to: '...the four project theorems reduce to a small set of elementary Mathlib lemmas. Each theorem's corresponding tests/test_jackknife.py test name is listed in §2.' OR re-author figure with badges.

advisories.description: Plotly Table figure has substantial blank space below the table (table fills upper ~30% of canvas). Cosmetic only — wastes column inches in JOSS layout. Crop or set tighter Plotly figure dimensions.
advisories.remediation: Reduce Plotly figure 'height' parameter or set explicit margins to crop the unused lower region. No content change needed.

advisories.description: Header column reads 'Verlinde verified' but caption phrasing is 'Verlinde-formula' (paper line 518). Minor copy-edit consistency issue. Either rename column header to 'Verlinde-formula verified' or update caption to use 'Verlinde verified'.
advisories.remediation: One-token edit in either the figure regeneration script or the caption.

advisories.description: Sankey labels are legible at 0.95\linewidth in single-column layout but borderline in two-column. If JOSS submission is intended at single-column, this is fine; if a CPC two-column variant is anticipated, increase Plotly font size or split into two sub-figures (foundations + instances).
advisories.remediation: Verify target journal column layout; adjust Plotly font_size if two-column.

advisories.description: Round-1 I2-FIG-1 recommendation explicitly listed 'SphericalCategory' as a library-original tier to surface as a node. Current figure omits SphericalCategory as a separate node; the §3 prose treats it as part of the Pivotal->Ribbon chain. Strengthening pass could add SphericalCategory as a node between Pivotal and Ribbon (or as a parallel branch off Pivotal) for completeness.
advisories.remediation: Optional. Either add SphericalCategory node to fig 1, or update §3 prose to explicitly state 'SphericalCategory.lean is treated as the pivotal-spherical specialization stage and is not shown as a separate node in Fig 1'.

next_actions: REQUIRED: Re-author papers/I2/figures/i2_fig5_mathlib_upstream_flow.png to fix label clipping; re-invoke physics-qa:figure-reviewer with bundle_target=I2 for verification.
Reconcile i2_fig4 caption (paper line 252-264) with the figure: either drop per-theorem test-annotation claim or add test-name badges to each theorem node.
Optional cleanup: crop fig 3 vertical extent; rename fig 3 column header for caption-consistency; consider adding SphericalCategory node to fig 1.
Once fig 5 fixed, expect overall_status to upgrade from 'issues_found' to 'pass' and bundle_metadata.stage9_status from 'yellow' to 'green'.
